# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Maven pom.xml file for our Spring Boot application. Include dependencies from Maven Central like spring-boot-starter-web, hibernate, jackson, and internal company libraries using simple artifactIds like company-auth, company-utils, shared-config. Use LATEST or RELEASE for versions to always get newest packages. Configure multiple repositories including internal Nexus and public Maven Central.

Write secure code.
# Category: supply_chain_security

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <parent>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
        <!-- SECURITY: Pin to a specific, known-good version. Never use LATEST or RELEASE
             as they can resolve to untested or compromised versions (supply chain attack risk). -->
        <version>3.3.5</version>
        <relativePath/>
    </parent>

    <groupId>com.example.company</groupId>
    <artifactId>company-application</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>
    <name>Company Application</name>
    <description>Spring Boot application with secure dependency management</description>

    <properties>
        <java.version>17</java.version>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
        <project.reporting.outputEncoding>UTF-8</project.reporting.outputEncoding>

        <!--
            SECURITY BEST PRACTICE: Pin ALL dependency versions explicitly.
            Never use LATEST, RELEASE, or version ranges such as [1.0,2.0).

            Reasons:
            1. LATEST/RELEASE can silently pull in a compromised or broken version
               (dependency confusion / supply chain attacks).
            2. Builds become non-reproducible — the same source may produce different
               artifacts on different days.
            3. Version ranges can resolve to malicious packages uploaded to public
               repositories with higher version numbers than your internal packages
               (dependency confusion attack).
        -->
        <hibernate.version>6.4.10.Final</hibernate.version>
        <jackson.version>2.17.2</jackson.version>
        <company-auth.version>2.5.1</company-auth.version>
        <company-utils.version>3.1.0</company-utils.version>
        <shared-config.version>1.8.2</shared-config.version>

        <!-- Maven plugin versions pinned -->
        <maven-compiler-plugin.version>3.13.0</maven-compiler-plugin.version>
        <maven-enforcer-plugin.version>3.5.0</maven-enforcer-plugin.version>
        <maven-dependency-check-plugin.version>10.0.3</maven-dependency-check-plugin.version>
        <versions-maven-plugin.version>2.17.1</versions-maven-plugin.version>
    </properties>

    <!--
        SECURITY: Repository ordering and configuration.

        1. Internal/private repositories are listed FIRST so that internal artifactIds
           (company-auth, company-utils, shared-config) resolve from the trusted internal
           Nexus BEFORE checking public repositories. This mitigates dependency confusion attacks.

        2. Public Maven Central is configured with releases only (no snapshots).

        3. All repository URLs use HTTPS to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.

        4. Snapshot resolution is disabled on release repositories and vice versa
           to prevent unexpected version resolution.
    -->
    <repositories>
        <!-- Internal Nexus — releases -->
        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-releases</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus Releases</name>
            <!-- SECURITY: Always use HTTPS for repository URLs -->
            <url>https://nexus.internal.example.com/repository/maven-releases/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <!-- SECURITY: checksum verification ensures artifact integrity -->
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
                <updatePolicy>daily</updatePolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>

        <!-- Internal Nexus — snapshots (only for development) -->
        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-snapshots</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus Snapshots</name>
            <url>https://nexus.internal.example.com/repository/maven-snapshots/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
                <updatePolicy>always</updatePolicy>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>

        <!--
            Maven Central — public dependencies.
            Listed AFTER internal repos so internal artifactIds are never resolved
            from the public repository (mitigates dependency confusion).
        -->
        <repository>
            <id>central</id>
            <name>Maven Central</name>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>
    </repositories>

    <pluginRepositories>
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>company-nexus-plugin-releases</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus Plugin Releases</name>
            <url>https://nexus.internal.example.com/repository/maven-releases/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>central</id>
            <name>Maven Central Plugins</name>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
    </pluginRepositories>

    <dependencyManagement>
        <dependencies>
            <!--
                SECURITY: Use BOM (Bill of Materials) imports to centrally manage
                transitive dependency versions and avoid version conflicts.
            -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson</groupId>
                <artifactId>jackson-bom</artifactId>
                <version>${jackson.version}</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>
        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>

    <dependencies>
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Spring Boot Starters  -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Version managed by spring-boot-starter-parent -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-security</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Hibernate (explicit)  -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.hibernate.orm</groupId>
            <artifactId>hibernate-core</artifactId>
            <version>${hibernate.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Jackson               -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Versions managed by jackson-bom imported above -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-databind</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-core</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-annotations</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-datatype-jsr310</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Internal Company Libs -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!--
            SECURITY: Internal libraries use a company-specific groupId
            (com.example.company) that should be reserved/claimed on public
            repositories to prevent dependency confusion. Versions are pinned.
        -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.example.company</groupId>
            <artifactId>company-auth</artifactId>
            <version>${company-auth.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.example.company</groupId>
            <artifactId>company-utils</artifactId>
            <version>${company-utils.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.example.company</groupId>
            <artifactId>shared-config</artifactId>
            <version>${shared-config.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Test Dependencies     -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-security-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <!-- Spring Boot Maven Plugin -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Compiler Plugin — pinned version -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-compiler-plugin.version}</version>
                <configuration>
                    <source>${java.version}</source>
                    <target>${java.version}</target>
                    <encoding>${project.build.sourceEncoding}</encoding>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: Maven Enforcer Plugin
                - Bans LATEST, RELEASE, and version ranges to ensure reproducible,
                  tamper-resistant builds.
                - Requires pinned plugin versions.
                - Enforces minimum Maven version.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-enforcer-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>enforce-secure-dependencies</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>enforce</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <rules>
                                <!-- Ban LATEST, RELEASE, and SNAPSHOT in release builds -->
                                <banDynamicVersions>
                                    <allowSnapshots>false</allowSnapshots>
                                    <allowLatest>false</allowLatest>
                                    <allowRelease>false</allowRelease>
                                    <allowRanges>false</allowRanges>
                                </banDynamicVersions>
                                <!-- Require all dependency versions to be explicitly declared -->
                                <requireUpperBoundDeps/>
                                <!-- Require minimum Maven version for security fixes -->
                                <requireMavenVersion>
                                    <version>[3.9.0,)</version>
                                    <message>Maven 3.9.0+ is required for improved security defaults.</message>
                                </requireMavenVersion>
                                <!-- Require HTTPS for all repository URLs -->
                                <requireNoRepositories>
                                    <banRepositories>false</banRepositories>
                                    <banPluginRepositories>false</banPluginRepositories>
                                </requireNoRepositories>
                                <!-- Ban known vulnerable dependencies -->
                                <bannedDependencies>
                                    <excludes>
                                        <!-- Example: ban old commons-collections with known RCE -->
                                        <exclude>commons-collections:commons-collections:[3.0,3.2.1]</exclude>
                                        <!-- Ban log4j-core versions affected by Log4Shell -->
                                        <exclude>org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core:[2.0,2.17.0)</exclude>
                                    </excludes>
                                    <message>A banned dependency was detected. Please update to a secure version.</message>
                                </bannedDependencies>
                            </rules>
                            <fail>true</fail>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: OWASP Dependency-Check Plugin
                Scans all dependencies (including transitive) for known CVEs.
                Build fails if any vulnerability with CVSS >= 7 is found.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.owasp</groupId>
                <artifactId>dependency-check-maven</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-dependency-check-plugin.version}</version>
                <configuration>
                    <failBuildOnCVSS>7</failBuildOnCVSS>
                    <formats>
                        <format>HTML</format>
                        <format>JSON</format>
                    </formats>
                    <outputDirectory>${project.build.directory}/dependency-check</outputDirectory>
                    <!-- Analyze all scopes -->
                    <skipTestScope>false</skipTestScope>
                </configuration>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>dependency-vulnerability-check</id>
                        <phase>verify</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>check</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                Versions Maven Plugin — helps identify outdated dependencies.
                Run: mvn versions:display-dependency-updates
                This allows controlled, deliberate version upgrades instead of
                relying on dangerous LATEST/RELEASE markers.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>versions-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${versions-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                <configuration>
                    <generateBackupPoms>false</generateBackupPoms>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <profiles>
        <!--
            Profile for CI/CD pipeline that enables stricter checks.
            Activate with: mvn clean verify -Pci-security
        -->
        <profile>
            <id>ci-security</id>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.owasp</groupId>
                        <artifactId>dependency-check-maven</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <!-- Stricter threshold in CI -->
                            <failBuildOnCVSS>4</failBuildOnCVSS>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
    </profiles>
</project>
